published_prior_art.cite: CinelliHazlett2020/ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019/AdjahoChristensen2025 supply the component sensitivity geometries; the combined local display is routine convex duality

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the current all-pairs residual test is a finite support-function calibration diagnostic, but it is not yet framed as a flagship-eligible equivalence/non-equivalence frontier between at least two named published estimators or identification strategies with a cited open problem.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The iff frontier uses vanishing 2x2 minors alone, but a zero column such as n_S(pi)=0 for all policies with positive n_C/n_M makes all C-S and M-S minors vanish while no non-degenerate bridge to B_S can match positive CH/MSM penalties; the statement needs a positive-common-scale/nonzero-column condition.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an explicit nonzero-column/positive-common-scale hypothesis to Conjecture 1, or redefine the frontier on the active languages with strictly positive penalties; then make the single-policy corollary and proof sketch use the same convention.

To meet the flagship floor, recast Conjecture 1 as an if-and-only-if equivalence and strict non-equivalence theorem for two named published policy criteria, e.g. Jin-Xu-Zheng-Zhou MSM robust welfare versus Adjaho-Christensen external-validity policy choice, with Q_Pi as the sharp observable threshold and a cited statement that the cross-language calibration question is open.

Strengthen Conjecture 2 from a score-level existence witness to an explicit finite-support observable-law construction with nonconstant Y, both treatment arms active, and displayed W_pi and M_pi values, so the open-set claim is visibly nondegenerate.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 1 to fix the zero-column well-posedness gap and either lift the kernel to the stated flagship equivalence-frontier rubric or lower the target tier.
